message:
test(conformance): add fixture tests for 15 frankenlibc harness families + backtrace_ops

Continue the per-family fixture-test rollout (cf. recent socket_ops,
unistd_ops, string_ops, string_memory_full, wide_string_ops/wide_memory,
wide_string, strlen_strict/memcpy_strict commits).

New harness fixture-test files in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/
(40-ish lines each, parameterized over `strict` / `hardened` modes
where applicable):

- ctype_ops_conformance_test.rs (38)
- dirent_ops_conformance_test.rs (38)
- errno_ops_conformance_test.rs (52)
- poll_ops_conformance_test.rs (38)
- pressure_sensing_conformance_test.rs (57)
- resource_ops_conformance_test.rs (38)
- signal_ops_conformance_test.rs (38)
- startup_ops_conformance_test.rs (42)
- stdlib_conversion_conformance_test.rs (40)
- string_memory_full_conformance_test.rs (40)
- string_ops_conformance_test.rs (40)
- sysv_ipc_ops_conformance_test.rs (50)
- wide_memory_conformance_test.rs (40)
- wide_string_conformance_test.rs (40)
- wide_string_ops_conformance_test.rs (40)

Each test loads the matching tests/conformance/fixtures/<family>.json,
walks every case through `execute_fixture_case`, and asserts the
`expected_output` matches across the configured parser modes.

Also adds an in-crate sibling test in
crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:

- `backtrace_ops_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case`: deserializes
  the backtrace_ops fixture, skips the `truncated_count` corruption
  scenario (requires a simulated corrupt stack), and validates every
  remaining case under both strict/hardened modes (or the case-pinned
  mode when not "both").

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
